rows.title: Silent Book Club
rows.description:
Bring your own book and read together quietly.

6-6:30 Meet the group and see what everyone brought

6:30-7:30 Reading silently

7:30-8 Chat and hang out! Talk about the book you read or just make a new friend.

rows.title: Summer Maker's Fair
rows.description:
Drop in for handmade gifts, art, and flash tattoos at our summer maker's fair!


Pottery by @shamelessceramics For Hannah (she/they), pottery is the thing that brought her back to life. Shameless Ceramics is a project built on learning patience and to accept the imperfect as just part of the art. Hannah is both intimidated and inspired by the endless ways to create with clay, and is really just happy to be another queer artist doing the damn thing.

Sterling Silver Jewelry and Oddities by @emstreasure Em's Treasure is a collection of precious metal jewelry and a handful of oddities. Em (they/she) is a beachcomber and rockhounder at heart, often sourcing crystals, shells, bones, and plants from the lush mountains and beaches of the PNW.

Vintage and Upcycled Jewelry by @secondslicestudio Second Slice Studio features whimsical, one of a kind wearables and home decor made from upcycled, vintage, and salvaged jewelry. Every piece, from necklaces to jewelry boxes to keepsake frames, tells a unique story of each element’s history, highlighting the magic and beauty of forgotten things. Second Slice Studio marries maximalism with sustainability - so you can reduce waste and still live your fairy-princess-space queen-y2k diva-wizard fantasy!

Flash Tattoos by @fastelsatattoos Elsa (they/them) is a working artist who has somehow ended up with five jobs because they don't know how to stop doing a craft once they've started it, and would've been a really hot peasant. As a tattooer with 5+ years of experience, they're interested in illustrative work, both highly detailed and stupidly simple. Their practice is rooted in somatic experience and the healing and transformative capacity of tattoos, especially for queer and trans communities.

Live Poetry by @uv.ube ULTRAVIOLET Poetry aims to lean into vulnerability, moving through spaces of spontaneous moments by sharing pay-what-you-wish poems for folks on a typewriter: poems are based on a topic, a free write, or a card reading. A creative space for COY (they/them/siya), who is inspired by their Fil-Am background, queerness, called upon by ancestors & spirit guides.

Stained Glass by @murder.shed.glassworks Dyl Knight (they/them) is an emerging stained glass artist. They are particularly drawn to queer subjects, medieval imagery, and the natural world.You can find more of their work on Instagram at murder.shed.glassworks.

Prints and Original Artwork by @thescorwin Sarah Corwin is mixed-media painter and collage artist. Her work focuses on cityscapes, oceans, and natural disasters, processed through a surreal, imaginative lens. She sells prints of her art at her pop-up art stand, Corwin’s Storewin.

Zines and Printed Works by @charliett CharlieTtea (they/them) is a trans disabled artist/zinester living and working on Duwamish land. They make zines about gender, disability, mental health, and more. They enjoy being able to connect and find community through zines.

rows.title: Heightened Scrutiny Screening
rows.description:
RSVP here!

Heightened Scrutiny follows Chase Strangio, ACLU attorney and the first out trans person to argue before the Supreme Court, as he fights a high-stakes legal battle to overturn Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for transgender youth (United States v. Skrmetti). The film exposes the dangerous role of mainstream media in fueling anti-trans legislation, uncovering how biased coverage drives hate, endangers lives, and threatens democracy itself. With insights from journalists like Jelani Cobb, Lydia Polgreen, and Gina Chua, and activists like Laverne Cox, the story dismantles anti-trans disinformation and highlights its devastating real-world impact. With the dangerous SCOTUS decision upholding the ban on life-saving healthcare, Heightened Scrutiny is an urgent call to action against bigotry and injustice.

This screening is presented by the Trans Journalists Association and Charlie's Queer Books. It is FREE with RSVP. Donations are encouraged and benefit the Trans Journalists Association.

About the Trans Journalists Association: The Trans Journalists Association is a nonprofit professional membership organization that promotes accurate, nuanced coverage of trans rights and communities in the media and fosters the careers of gender-expansive journalists. Founded in 2020, the TJA is building a future of journalism where stories about trans people are just, ethical, and informed by trans leadership.

rows.title: This Book Group is Queer (virtual)
rows.description:
We've partnered with King County Library to create a virtual theme-based book club! Find conversation and community in a lively LGBTQIA+ book chat on the 2nd Tuesday of the month.

This month's theme is Sapphic with Swords!🤺 For a list of reading suggestions on this month's theme, click here.

👉RSVP here to get the Zoom link👈 

Pro tip: Sign up for the waitlist! Odds are very high that you'll get the link ;)

rows.title: Becky Chambers 'As You Wake, Break the Shell'
rows.description:
✨ GET TICKETS HERE! ✨ All tickets include a copy of the book.

The exponential advances in technology in the 21st Century are accompanied by increasingly prescient structural and social ills, losses of human connection, and a cripplingly bleak outlook on the future to come. How can we persist in a society which seeks to separate us from each other, forcing us to operate in an environment that is antithetical to empathy, togetherness, and true contentment?

In her new book, As You Wake, Break the Shell, Hugo Award-winning author, Becky Chambers, grapples with these issues. Holding a mirror to our own society, Chambers depicts a universe of stubborn survival and found family, and of persistent danger and quiet wonder. As You Wake takes place on the resource-poor planet of Fortune, where life is challenging — but for its inhabitants, it’s home. Signy is a brilliant but reclusive botanist who bio-prints medicine from her small plant shop, often bending the rules to help those whom the system is designed to leave behind. Cora is a pilot, one of the few with the ability to navigate the massive, living ships that connect the scattered settlements of their solar system. Always looming is the threat of the “mindbleed,” a dangerous side effect of piloting that brings her to Signy’s shop. What ensues is a tale of humanity, romance, and situation-defying persistence. 

Told in an alternating timeline of their initial connection and a not-so-distant future where they have found domestic bliss, As You Wake is a story of harrowing yet hopeful determination. Chambers offers readers a vivid glimpse into what it means to be human, and how we can foster meaningful connection in spite of our circumstances. Come gather in the Forum at Town Hall Seattle to hear Chambers discuss her new work, and be amongst those who believe in the power of fiction to show us a way forward, even in the bleakest of times.

Becky Chambers is the bestselling author of the Wayfarers series, the Monk and Robot novellas, and other works of science fiction. She is a two-time Hugo Award winner, a Locus Award winner, and has been nominated for the Nebula Award, the Arthur C. Clarke Award, and the Women’s Prize for Fiction, among others. Her latest work is the upcoming novel As You Wake, Break the Shell, the first in a new duology. Becky has a background in performing arts, and grew up in a family heavily involved in space science. She spends her free time playing video games, hanging out in the woods, and learning about the universe. She still hopes to see Earth from orbit one day, though she’s gotten picky about who she’d take a ride from. Prone to hopping around, Becky can be found either in her home state of California or her wife’s home country of Iceland.
